assertions.text: Recommends posting a design email to pgsql-hackers first for a non-trivial GUC (not skipping to v1)
Names exact target list: pgsql-hackers@lists.postgresql.org
Specifies plain text only, patch as attachment (not inline)
Gives exact git format-patch -v1 master..HEAD command
Mentions git diff --check master..HEAD for whitespace
Names the current open CommitFest (PG20-1) explicitly
States the review-bartering norm: review at least one other patch in the same CF
Names the SGML doc location for a GUC (doc/src/sgml/config.sgml)
Tells user to reply on the existing thread, NOT start a new one
Bumps version with git format-patch -v2 master..HEAD
Says to flip CF entry from Waiting on Author back to Needs Review
Advises folding fixes into logical commits via rebase -i (not adding 'fix review' commit on top)
Mentions disagreements should be answered inline rather than silently ignored
Specifies plain text email, attachment not inline, no top-posting
Warns that stalled patches get Returned with Feedback or Moved to next CF
Confirms a small bug fix STILL goes via pgsql-hackers + CommitFest (no shortcut)
Says you can skip the design-thread step for an obvious bug fix
Says to develop the fix against master, not back branches (backpatch is committer's job)
Lists the four backpatch criteria (frequently-encountered / low-risk / security / data-corruption)
Names the current open CommitFest (PG20-1)
Mentions reviewing one other patch in the CF (bartering norm) still applies
Lists at least three anti-checklist items (no HTML, attachment not inline, no top-posting, no corp footer, whitespace clean)
